name: 80s Pop Culture
description: Music, movies, events, and trends from the 1980s

questions.q: Who, in 1981, became the first woman appointed to the U.S. Supreme Court?

source.notes: Nominated by Reagan, confirmed 99-0. Served until 2006.

questions.q: Which cable channel launched in August 1981 with the slogan 'You'll never look at music the same way again'?

source.notes: First video aired: 'Video Killed the Radio Star' by The Buggles.

questions.q: Who became the first American woman to travel to space in 1983?

source.notes: Aboard STS-7 on Space Shuttle Challenger. She was 32 years old.

questions.q: Sony introduced which portable music player in 1979-1980, letting consumers listen privately on the go?

source.notes: Launched in Japan July 1979, US mid-1980. Sold over 200 million units worldwide.

questions.q: Who became President of the United States in January 1981?

source.notes: 40th President. Defeated Carter in 1980 election with 489 electoral votes.

questions.q: Which arcade game, released by Namco in 1980, features a yellow character eating dots in a maze?

source.notes: Originally named 'Puck-Man.' The ghosts are Blinky, Pinky, Inky, and Clyde.

questions.q: Which plush doll craze swept the US in 1983, with each one coming with its own unique name and 'adoption papers'?

source.notes: Created by Xavier Roberts. Caused shopping riots during the 1983 holiday season.

questions.q: Michael Jackson released his record-shattering album 'Thriller' in which year?

source.notes: Released November 30, 1982. Best-selling album of all time (~70 million copies).

questions.q: Which hard rock band released the 1987 debut album 'Appetite for Destruction'?

source.notes: Best-selling debut in US history (~30M copies). Features 'Sweet Child o' Mine,' 'Welcome to the Jungle.'

questions.q: Which AC/DC album, released in 1980, features the title track about the band's late singer?

source.notes: Tribute to Bon Scott. Second best-selling album of all time worldwide (~50M).

questions.q: Bonnie Tyler's 1983 hit 'Total Eclipse of the Heart' was written and produced by which rock songwriter?

source.notes: Steinman also wrote Meat Loaf's 'Bat Out of Hell' album.

questions.q: What was the name of the sneaky AI computer in the 1983 film 'WarGames'?

source.notes: Also known as the WOPR. Starred Matthew Broderick.

questions.q: Who directed 'E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial' (1982), the highest-grossing film of the 1980s?

source.notes: Grossed $792M worldwide. Held highest-grossing title until 'Jurassic Park' (1993).

questions.q: Who played Jack Torrance in Stanley Kubrick's 1980 horror film 'The Shining'?

source.notes: Famous line: 'Here's Johnny!' — improvised by Nicholson.

questions.q: Who played NYPD detective John McClane in the 1988 action film 'Die Hard'?

source.notes: Adapted from the novel 'Nothing Lasts Forever.' Set at Nakatomi Plaza on Christmas Eve.

questions.q: Who played Axel Foley in the 1984 comedy 'Beverly Hills Cop'?

source.notes: Highest-grossing comedy of the 1980s. Sylvester Stallone was originally cast.

questions.q: Which Richard Attenborough biopic won the Academy Award for Best Picture for the year 1982?

source.notes: Won 8 Oscars including Best Actor for Ben Kingsley.

questions.q: In 'The Breakfast Club,' Molly Ringwald plays 'the princess.' What is her character's name?

source.notes: Directed by John Hughes. Set at Shermer High School in Illinois.

questions.q: Which NFL team won Super Bowl XX in January 1986, a season marked by their famous 'Super Bowl Shuffle' music video?

source.notes: Defeated New England Patriots 46-10. Coach: Mike Ditka. QB: Jim McMahon.

questions.q: Which 1980s crime drama featured pastel-suited Miami detectives Sonny Crockett and Ricardo Tubbs?

source.notes: Played by Don Johnson and Philip Michael Thomas. Aired 1984-1989 on NBC.
